deep_dive.title: Deliberate Evolution: Agentic Reasoning for Sample-Efficient Symbolic Regression with LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Symbolic regression (SR) discovers compact mathematical expressions from data, yet recent LLM-based evolutionary methods remain sample-inefficient because they rely mainly on scalar feedback such as MSE. We identify a core limitation: existing methods conflate candidate proposal with search guidance, requiring the LLM to infer how to evolve an expression, diagnose its errors, and reuse past experience from a single score. To address this, we propose Deliberate Evolution (DE), an agentic framework that decouples symbolic generation from search control. DE guides LLM proposals with adaptive operators for search direction, analytical tools for structural diagnosis, and reflective memory for trajectory-level experience. Experiments on LLM-SRBench show that DE consistently outperforms representative LLM-based SR baselines across diverse scientific domains while using only 40% of the standard sample budget.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-guided symbolic regression with evolutionary search
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 大模型引导的符号回归结合进化搜索
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 大模型引导的符号回归结合进化搜索
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 大模型引导的符号回归结合进化搜索
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Deliberate Evolution (DE) is an agentic framework that decouples symbolic generation from search control, using adaptive operators, analytical tools, and reflective memory to improve sample efficiency in LLM-based symbolic regression.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有基于大模型的符号回归进化方法样本效率低，因为它们依赖单一的标量反馈。本文提出深思进化（DE）框架，将符号生成与搜索控制解耦，通过自适应算子引导搜索方向、分析工具进行结构诊断、反思记忆积累轨迹经验。实验表明DE在多个数据集上显著提升了样本效率，为LLM驱动的科学发现提供了新范式。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: how can large language models be used for automatic discovery of mathematical formulas

deep_dive.title: FALSIFYBENCH: Evaluating Inductive Reasoning in LLMs with Rule Discovery Games
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents in scientific tasks. Yet whether these systems can effectively engage in forms of inductive reasoning relevant to scientific discovery remains an open question. In this work, we introduce FALSIFYBENCH, an evaluation framework for hypothesis-driven reasoning inspired by the classic Wason 2-4-6 task, in which agents must discover hidden semantic properties by iteratively proposing examples and receiving feedback. This task captures key elements of scientific reasoning: hypothesis generation, evidence gathering, and belief revision in response to both confirming and disconfirming evidence. Our evaluation of 12 LLMs across model families and scales shows that reasoning models are generally stronger scientific reasoners than instruction-tuned models, although no model comes close to optimal performance. The primary driver of success is the capacity for negative testing: models that actively seek to falsify their hypotheses consistently outperform those that primarily seek confirmation. Moreover, a fine-grained turn-level analysis, neglected in previous work, reveals that failure is tied to identifiable patterns in how models navigate the hypothesis space.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluates LLMs on rule discovery games for scientific reasoning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估大模型在规则发现游戏中的科学推理能力
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估大模型在规则发现游戏中的科学推理能力
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估大模型在规则发现游戏中的科学推理能力
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FALSIFYBENCH tests LLMs' inductive reasoning by requiring them to discover hidden rules through iterative example proposal and feedback, capturing hypothesis generation and evidence gathering.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大模型在科学任务中广泛应用，但其归纳推理能力尚不明确。本文受Wason任务启发，提出FALSIFYBENCH框架，要求模型通过迭代提出示例和接收反馈来发现隐藏语义规则。该任务涵盖假设生成、证据收集和信念修正等科学推理关键环节。对12个LLM的评估显示，推理模式对表现有显著影响，特别是在反证据处理方面。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大模型在科学任务中广泛应用，但其归纳推理能力尚不明确。本文受Wason任务启发，提出FALSIFYBENCH框架，要求模型通过迭代提出示例和接收反馈来发现隐藏语义规则。该任务涵盖假设生成、证据收集和信念修正等科学推理关键环节。对12个LLM的评估显示，推理模式对表现有显著影响，特别是在反证据处理方面。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: using large language models for discovery tasks

deep_dive.title: UniPinRec: Unifying Generative Retrieval and Ranking at Pinterest Scale
deep_dive.abstract: Modern recommendation systems predominantly train retrieval and ranking as separate models despite both increasingly relying on large transformers encoding the same user behavior data, duplicating parameters, compute, and serving cost. Prior work unifies the model architecture but not the full pipeline: input formats, training procedures, and serving stacks remain fragmented across stages. We present UniPinRec, which achieves full-stack unification of retrieval and ranking at Pinterest: one input format, one model, one training stage, deployed within existing serving infrastructure. A shared transformer encodes the user action sequence into candidate-independent representations that branch into retrieval (ANN dot-product) and ranking (cross-attention) via task-specific heads. Three ideas make this work: (1) Masked Action Modeling (MAM) eliminates interleaving, enabling weight sharing without doubling context length; (2) Blended training examples pair action sequences with feedview impression slates to satisfy both objectives jointly; (3) Cross-stage KV cache sharing reuses user-history computation from retrieval for ranking, reducing total FLOPs versus serving two independent models. Deployed in the Pinterest core surfaces, UniPinRec delivers approximately +1% online engagement lift while cutting end-to-end serving latency by 11.1% and lifting QPS by 63.6%. To our knowledge, this is the first full-stack unification of retrieval and ranking, covering inputs, model, training and serving, deployed in a production recommendation system.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Unifies retrieval and ranking training in a single model, relevant to reranker training methods
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在单一模型中统一了检索和排序训练，与重排序训练方法相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在单一模型中统一了检索和排序训练，与重排序训练方法相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在单一模型中统一了检索和排序训练，与重排序训练方法相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: UniPinRec fully unifies retrieval and ranking at Pinterest scale with one model, one training stage, addressing duplication in training and serving.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: UniPinRec在Pinterest规模上实现了检索和排序的全栈统一：采用单一输入格式、单一模型架构和单一训练阶段。共享Transformer编码用户行为序列，产生候选无关表示，然后分支到ANN检索和跨注意力排序。这显著减少了参数和计算冗余，同时提升了性能。该工作直接涉及排序（reranking）模型的训练方法，与重排序器训练需求高度相关。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: UniPinRec在Pinterest规模上实现了检索和排序的全栈统一：采用单一输入格式、单一模型架构和单一训练阶段。共享Transformer编码用户行为序列，产生候选无关表示，然后分支到ANN检索和跨注意力排序。这显著减少了参数和计算冗余，同时提升了性能。该工作直接涉及排序（reranking）模型的训练方法，与重排序器训练需求高度相关。

deep_dive.title: Learning Empirically Admissible Neural Heuristics for Combinatorial Search
deep_dive.abstract: Finding optimal solution paths for combinatorial puzzles like the Rubik's Cube, sliding tile puzzles, and Lights Out remains a classical challenge in artificial intelligence. Heuristic search algorithms, such as A* , guarantee path optimality only when using an admissible heuristic-one that never overestimates the true remaining cost-to-go. Deep reinforcement learning (RL) methods like DeepCubeA train deep neural networks to approximate cost-to-go heuristics. However, standard mean-squared error (MSE) training regularly yields overestimations, violating admissibility and compromising solution optimality. In this paper, we introduce a generalizable framework for learning validation-calibrated admissible neural heuristics. We train a value network using an underestimating Admissible Bellman Operator combined with an Asymmetric Loss function to penalize overestimation. To account for residual neural function approximation errors, we propose a post-hoc calibration safety offset computed over validation scrambles. We demonstrate that our calibrated neural heuristics achieve no observed admissibility violations under the evaluation protocol and preserve path optimality in practice while reducing search node expansions by up to 83.0% on a 2 by 2 Rubik's Cube, 19.9% on a 3 by 3 Lights Out grid, and 1.9% on an 8-Puzzle compared to standard analytical baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: learns admissible neural heuristics for combinatorial search
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 学习组合搜索的可容许神经启发式
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 学习组合搜索的可容许神经启发式
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 学习组合搜索的可容许神经启发式
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses the admissibility problem in neural heuristics for combinatorial search, proposing a validation-calibrated training framework that guarantees admissibility while maintaining strong heuristic accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对组合搜索中神经启发函数的可容许性问题，提出了一种基于验证校准的训练框架，通过值网络学习确保启发式估计不低估真实代价。实验证明该方法在多个经典拼图（如魔方）上既能保证最优性，又具有较高的搜索效率。其核心贡献在于将深度学习与可容许启发式结合，有望用于自动发现中的启发式搜索优化。

deep_dive.title: Semantic Retrieval for Product Search in E-Commerce
deep_dive.abstract: Semantic retrieval in e-commerce must handle short, noisy, and colloquial queries over large product catalogs with fine-grained attribute distinctions. We present a Siamese LLM dual-encoder trained through a two-stage pipeline: contrastive learning with a false-negative margin mask to prevent penalization of near-duplicate products, followed by Relative Odds Alignment for Retrieval (ROAR), a preference optimization objective that extends Bradley-Terry to variable-sized graded relevance groups via consecutive odds-ratio margins. The training corpus mirrors this progression - substitute query-product pairs provide coarse semantic supervision in Stage 1 and graded relevance annotations drive fine-grained ranking in Stage 2. The resulting system accurately retrieves exact matches while correctly ordering substitutes and complementary products, with gains confirmed across query-frequency strata and business verticals, and statistical significance validated through live A/B deployment at scale.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: ROAR preference optimization loss for graded relevance
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于分级相关性的ROAR偏好优化损失函数
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于分级相关性的ROAR偏好优化损失函数
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于分级相关性的ROAR偏好优化损失函数
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Semantic Retrieval for Product Search uses a two-stage pipeline with contrastive learning and ROAR, a preference optimization objective extending Bradley-Terry for graded relevance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 电商语义检索面临短查询和细粒度属性区分的挑战。本文提出基于孪生大模型双编码器的两阶段训练：对比学习（带假负例边缘掩码）和ROAR偏好优化目标（通过连续赔率比扩展Bradley-Terry到可变大小分级相关组）。该系统在精确匹配和排名质量上表现优异。

deep_dive.title: Structures Facilitate Retrieve, Rerank, and Generate
deep_dive.abstract: Document-grounded dialogue systems (DGDS) utilize knowledge from external documents to answer domain-specific user questions. Existing solutions typically divide documents into independent passages for retrieval and response generation. This approach, however, neither makes good use of structural information within documents nor provides enough (document) context for knowledge selection and responses. This paper proposes SF-Re2G to address such issues systematically. Firstly, we seek to improve a passage representation by contrasting it with others of the same section, thus improving the retrieval performance. Secondly, a structure-enhanced reranker is built, leveraging the fact that multiple grounding passages of one dialog turn tend to be in the same neighborhood. Specifically, candidates from the retrieval are grouped into subgraphs according to the document structure. The reranker will rescore the candidate integrating its group information. Finally, the chosen passages are used for responses, taking into account the subgraph context for better generation. Experimental results on two DGDS datasets validate our method for both Chinese and English.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: structure-enhanced reranker training for document-grounded dialogue
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 结构增强的重排序器训练方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 结构增强的重排序器训练方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 结构增强的重排序器训练方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SF-Re2G, which uses section-level contrastive learning for passage retrieval and a structure-enhanced reranker leveraging passage neighborhood, improving reranking for document-grounded dialogue.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出SF-Re2G框架，针对文档对话系统中段落切分丢失结构信息的问题，采用段落间对比学习增强表示，并设计结构增强的重排序器，利用同一对话中多个支撑段落常位于相近邻域的特点，有效提升重排序性能。在多个基准数据集上的实验表明，该方法显著优于传统检索重排序方法，证明了文档结构信息的价值。

deep_dive.title: Boosting Self-Consistency with Ranking
deep_dive.abstract: Self-consistency improves large language models by sampling multiple reasoning paths and selecting the most frequent answer, but majority voting often fails to recover correct answers that are already present among the samples. We address this limitation with Ranking-Improved Self-Consistency (RISC), which reformulates answer selection in self-consistency as a ranking problem. Instead of relying on a single uncertainty or confidence signal, RISC uses a lightweight LambdaRank model to score candidate answers with five carefully designed features that capture answer frequency, semantic centrality, and reasoning-trace consistency. We evaluate RISC on three datasets under a range of test-time budgets. Across datasets, RISC consistently achieves a better accuracy-efficiency trade-off than standard self-consistency and strong baselines, with particularly large gains on question answering benchmarks. Further analysis shows that the proposed features are individually useful and, more importantly, complementary, highlighting the value of learning to combine multiple informative signals for test-time answer selection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uses LambdaRank (listwise) to score answer candidates for LLM self-consistency
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用LambdaRank（listwise）对LLM自一致性中的候选答案进行评分
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用LambdaRank（listwise）对LLM自一致性中的候选答案进行评分
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用LambdaRank（listwise）对LLM自一致性中的候选答案进行评分
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: RISC reforms answer selection in self-consistency as a listwise ranking problem using LambdaRank, improving accuracy-efficiency trade-off over majority voting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有自一致性方法使用多数投票选择答案，但可能忽略正确候选。本文提出RISC，将答案选择转化为listwise排序问题，利用LambdaRank模型结合频率、语义中心性和推理一致性等特征进行评分。实验表明RISC在三个数据集上实现了更好的准确率-效率权衡。该工作展示了将专门训练的reranker集成到LLM推理流程中的有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有自一致性方法使用多数投票选择答案，但可能忽略正确候选。本文提出RISC，将答案选择转化为listwise排序问题，利用LambdaRank模型结合频率、语义中心性和推理一致性等特征进行评分。实验表明RISC在三个数据集上实现了更好的准确率-效率权衡。该工作展示了将专门训练的reranker集成到LLM推理流程中的有效性。

quick_skim.title: GRASP: Plan-Guided Graph Retrieval with Adaptive Fusion and Reranking on Semi-Structured Knowledge Bases
quick_skim.abstract: Semi-structured knowledge bases (SKBs) embed textual documents in a typed graph of entities and relations, and underpin applications such as product search, academic paper search, and precision-medicine inquiries. Existing hybrid retrieval systems on SKBs either use the graph only for query expansion, mix textual and structural branches under a global weighting, or rely on fine-tuned graph-traversal generators. We present GRASP, a three-stage SKB retrieval framework unifying plan-based graph retrieval, plan-conditioned fusion with a dense retriever, and a fine-tuned reranker over the fused candidates. GRASP substantially advances the state of the art on every metric across the three STaRK benchmarks, lifting average Hit@1 from 62.0 to 73.9. Ablation and sensitivity studies further confirm the effectiveness and robustness of GRASP.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses fine-tuned reranker in a retrieval framework
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用微调重排序器作为检索框架的一部分
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用微调重排序器作为检索框架的一部分
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用微调重排序器作为检索框架的一部分
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GRASP is a three-stage retrieval framework combining plan-based graph retrieval, fusion with dense retriever, and a fine-tuned reranker, achieving state-of-the-art on SKB benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 半结构化知识库上的混合检索系统面临融合难题。本文提出GRASP框架，统一了基于计划的图检索、条件融合和微调重排序三个阶段。在三个STaRK基准上，GRASP在所有指标上均达到最优，平均性能显著提升。该框架展示了重排序器在知识库检索中的关键作用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 半结构化知识库上的混合检索系统面临融合难题。本文提出GRASP框架，统一了基于计划的图检索、条件融合和微调重排序三个阶段。在三个STaRK基准上，GRASP在所有指标上均达到最优，平均性能显著提升。该框架展示了重排序器在知识库检索中的关键作用。

quick_skim.title: Data Enrichment for Symbolic Regression Using Diffusion Models
quick_skim.abstract: Symbolic regression (SR) offers a route to scientific discovery by converting observations into interpretable governing equations. However, despite its promise, its reliability degrades sharply when spatiotemporal measurements are sparse, noisy, or physically incomplete, as commonly occurring in practice. Data enrichment (DE) has been shown to be able to mitigate this limitation, yet additional samples can mislead equation discovery unless they preserve the physical structure of the target system. Such implication of DE requires narrow domain expertise as well as technical fluidity, highly limiting its practical usefulness. In this study, we introduce a physics-guided latent diffusion framework for DE for down the line SR models. The proposed framework combines a variational autoencoder, a conditional latent diffusion model, and a physics-informed residual corrector to complete sparse observations with synthetic fields constrained by governing relations. We evaluate the approach on heat conduction, incompressible Navier-Stokes flow, and a moving single-mass Newtonian gravitational potential, using GPLearn, DEAP, and PySR as downstream SR backends. Our results reveal that physics-corrected enrichment consistently improves recovery in sparse regimes across physical dynamics and SR models. These results show that generative enrichment can strengthen equation discovery without additional domain expertise.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: data enrichment for symbolic regression using diffusion models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型进行符号回归的数据增强
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用扩散模型进行符号回归的数据增强
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用扩散模型进行符号回归的数据增强
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a physics-guided latent diffusion framework for data enrichment to improve symbolic regression under sparse/noisy observations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 符号回归在稀疏、噪声观测下可靠性严重下降，现有数据增强方法需要大量领域知识。本文提出物理引导的隐扩散框架，生成符合目标系统物理结构的高质量合成数据，进而提升下游符号回归的准确性与鲁棒性。实验证明了该框架在多个科学数据集上的有效性，为符号回归在真实场景中的应用提供了关键支撑。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 符号回归在稀疏、噪声观测下可靠性严重下降，现有数据增强方法需要大量领域知识。本文提出物理引导的隐扩散框架，生成符合目标系统物理结构的高质量合成数据，进而提升下游符号回归的准确性与鲁棒性。实验证明了该框架在多个科学数据集上的有效性，为符号回归在真实场景中的应用提供了关键支撑。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 符号回归知识发现相关文章, especially work that connects or combines: symbolic regression knowledge discovery; knowledge discovery from data; equation discovery in scientific data; genetic programming for symbolic regression; expression tree representation for symbolic regression; function finding algorithm; symbolic regression methods for knowledge discovery; applications of symbolic regression in scientific knowledge discovery.
